M3-FM-01: compare the same quantity measured with visibly different units
label both the count and the unit name
ask how big each unit is before accepting the count
M3-FM-02: use same-quantity examples with contrasting unit sizes
compare more units because they are smaller
sort examples where larger counts mean smaller units
M3-FM-03: compare one whole partitioned into different counts
use overlays or stacked models to compare part sizes
connect partition count to smaller part size
M3-FM-04: move the same principle across length area and sharing contexts
ask for the invariant idea before changing surface form
use parallel examples with different materials
